skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
stem: In a certain store, the standard width of a shelf is at least 1.2 meters and no more than 1.5 meters. A store manager wants to determine the number of shelves, n, that could be placed end-to-end along a wall that is 18 meters long, based on the standard width of a shelf. Which of the following describes all the possible values of n?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The maximum number of shelves occurs when the shelves are narrowest (1.2 m): 18/1.2 = 15. The minimum number occurs when the shelves are widest (1.5 m): 18/1.5 = 12. Therefore 12 ≤ n ≤ 15.

Choices B, C, and D are incorrect and may result from confusing the shelf-width range with the count range.
